Off Calendar Jun-05-2025 Set For Trial On Feb-23-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT As To Defendant Twiggy Tang
Off Calendar Jun-12-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Oliver M. Gold, Esq,. of PERKINS COIE LLP. Tel:310.788.9900 for plaintiff.
Samuel Ray, Esq., of Colla & Ray LLP. Tel: 415-579-1414 for defendnat Eric K. Chung.
No appearance for Defendant Counsel for TWIGGY TANG.

Hearing is held. Counsel for Defendant contests the tentative ruling concerning the amount of attorney's fees, asserting that the award should be reduced by $130,000. The matter was argued and discussed. Upon conclusion of the hearing, the Court adopts the tentative ruling with modifications.

1 - PLAINTIFF PREFERRED BANK's MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT As To Defendant Twiggy Tang.

Plaintiff Preferred Bank's application for writ of attachment as to defendant Twiggy Tang is granted. Plaintiff is entitled to the issuance of a writ of attachment in the amount of $4,112,196.04 against the identified property of Ms. Tang subject to the determination of Ms. Tang's claim of exemption. Counsel are required to meet and confer in an effort to reach agreement on or narrow the disputes as to Ms. Tang's claim of exemption.

The court exercises its discretion to consider this renewed application for a writ of attachment on a fuller evidentiary record. The court does not believe that plaintiff should be denied the ability to seek a writ of attachment merely because it failed to provide admissible evidence in its previous application.

All of Ms. Tang's substantive arguments opposing the issuance of the writ of attachment lack merit. The amount sought by plaintiff is fully supported by the evidence and is neither uncertain nor excessive. Ms. Tang's execution of the guaranty arose out of her conduct of a trade, business, or profession. The description of the property is sufficiently specific.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/HEK)

MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT /Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment - Notice To Defenat Eric K Chung
Off Calendar Mar-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1- PLAINTIFF PREFERRED BANK MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT Order To Defendant Eric K Chung.

Plaintiff Preferred Bank's application for right to attach order and writ of attachment as to defendant Eric Chung is off calendar per the request of the moving party.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)

MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT /Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment - Notice To Defendant Twiggy Tang
Off Calendar Mar-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - PLAINTIFF PREFERRED BANK MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT Order For Issuance To Defendant Twiggy Tang.

Plaintiff Preferred Bank's application for right to attach order and writ of attachment as to defendant Twiggy Tang is denied. A declaration supporting an attachment application must state facts with particularity and the declarant must show that he or she "can testify competently to the facts stated therein." (CCP 482.040.) The court sustains defendant Ms. Tang's objection to the declaration of Aaron Fan. (CCP 484.090(a)(2).) While Mr.Fan states that he is the Assistant Vice President of plaintiff and asserts that there were various breaches of the underlying loan by the borrower, he fails to establish a foundation for his assertions. Mr. Fan does not identify, much less attach, what, if any, records he relies upon for his assertions of breaches or whether those records satisfy the required elements for admissibility of business records. His failure to do so is in sharp contrast with the declarant in Lorber Industries v. Turbulence, Inc. (1985) 175 Cal. App, 3d 532, 536, a case that he relies on in his reply brief, who attached records that showed the key facts establishing entitlement to a right to attach order. Moreover, Mr. Fan does not even explain why Preferred Bak is entitled to the $7,769.92 in excess of the guaranty amount. (Royals v. Lu (2022) 81 Cal.App.5th 328, 350 [denying attachment where amount claimed uncertain].)

Order is signed.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)
